commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a sanitized tool-error summarizer, forwards the last tool-error summary through lifecycle metadata into the embedded TUI backend, and renders it on aborted TUI chat events with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +169. Total +217 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run for the reported Windows/vLLM loop, but the source path is high-confidence: validation emits detailed tool errors, current main records `lastToolError`, and the current TUI abort branch renders only `run aborted`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is appropriate because no concrete code defect was found; the next action is contributor real-behavior proof or a maintainer proof override.

target.title: fix(tui): show last tool-error summary on aborted runs
target.body:
## Summary

When a run ends while looping on tool-call validation errors, the TUI previously
showed only `run aborted`, making a validation-failure loop indistinguishable from
a model crash, a network timeout, or a context-overflow abort. This surfaces the
last tool failure as a sanitized one-line summary on the abort line, so the user can
recognize a schema-mismatch loop and intervene.

Closes #90982

## Problem

`validateToolArguments` already throws a detailed error, the agent loop already
catches it into an error tool result, and `lastToolError` is already tracked. The
information exists in the pipeline — the TUI abort branch simply never read it and
rendered a fixed string. This is a wiring gap, not a missing mechanism.

## Fix

Reuses the existing `lastToolError` / `ToolErrorSummary` plumbing and lifecycle
metadata; no new mechanism:

- `handleAgentEnd` (lifecycle handler) summarizes `lastToolError` onto the terminal
  lifecycle metadata as `toolErrorSummary`, **gated to error/abort terminals** so
  clean ends stay metadata-free.
- `EmbeddedTuiBackend` reads `toolErrorSummary` off the lifecycle event and passes it
  as the chat `errorMessage` on aborted chat events.
- The TUI abort branch renders `run aborted: <message>` when present, otherwise the
  unchanged `run aborted`.

**Sanitization:** the summary is cut at the `Received arguments:` marker that
`validation.ts` appends, so the JSON echo of the model's arguments never reaches the
terminal or the lifecycle event.

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **No retry-policy / loop-abort changes.** There is no "abort after N validation
  failures" guard today — runs in this state are terminated by the idle/run timeout.
  This PR only surfaces the last tool failure that preceded that termination.
- No tool-call parser or model changes.
- No new config option.

## Why no repeat count

The abort line omits an `N×` count by design. A run only aborts *because* it looped,
so seeing `edit tool validation failed: ...` on an abort already implies repetition.
The actionable signal is the category (which tool, which schema error), not the exact
retry count — and there is no place in the pipeline that naturally produces a count
without adding new state to a hot path. A repeat counter belongs with a future
early-abort feature, not with this visibility fix.

## Before / After

Before:

```
run aborted
```

After (single validation error):

```
run aborted: edit tool validation failed: edits: must have required properties edits
```

Multiple validation issues are joined on one line (`...: edits: must have required
properties edits; path: must be string`) and the whole summary is truncated at 160
chars.

## Reproduction note

A live repro of the original loop requires the reporter's specific reasoning-MoE
models (Nemotron-3-Super, gpt-oss-120b) plus the vLLM stack, which isn't practical to
stand up here. Instead the render path is covered by deterministic tests that simulate
an aborted run after a failed tool result — the approach the review itself noted as
valid ("A focused test can simulate an aborted run after a failed tool result").

## Testing

- `src/agents/tool-error-summary.test.ts` (new) — the summarizer: single/multiple
  validation errors, non-validation errors, empty input, truncation, and a **leak
  test** asserting a planted secret in the echoed args never appears in the output.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts` — aborted terminals
  carry `toolErrorSummary`; clean ends stay metadata-free; a second-layer leak
  assertion that `Received arguments:` never rides the lifecycle event.
- `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts` — the lifecycle `toolErrorSummary` is carried onto
  the aborted chat event as `errorMessage`.
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` — the abort line includes the summary when
  present and falls back to bare `run aborted` when absent.

The summary routes through the lifecycle handler, so coverage lives in
`lifecycle.test.ts`; the originally-suggested `payloads.errors.test.ts` is unchanged
and still passes.

Local run: **206 passed** (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`); `oxfmt --check` clean,
`oxlint` exit 0, `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:test` exit 0.
